$comment: knowgrph-acos-mcp-connector — AgentCore Runtime configuration. The `runtime`/`container` registration is authored by tasks 13.1/13.2 (thin MCP-forwarding adapter, decision 13.0); the `inboundAuth` block is the task 13.4 R15 Auth_Token reconciliation. No model provider keys appear anywhere (R11): the only runtime config is the control-plane endpoint + the server-side HS256 secret.
name: knowgrph-agentcore-mcp-forwarder

liveness.$comment: task 13.7 — open /ping liveness reconciled with R3.4/R15.6 /health.

cli.$comment: task 13.2 — AgentCore CLI (Node.js 20+) project registration, following the agentcore-samples `01-tutorials/01-AgentCore-runtime/02-hosting-MCP-server` pattern (a stateless streamable-HTTP MCP server on 0.0.0.0:8000/mcp, ARM64). The CLI tool and the container base image are version-pinned (no ^/~) to match the repo convention and keep `configure`/`launch` reproducible. configure/launch/invoke are OPERATOR-run and `cloud-deploy`-gated (task 13.9); NOTHING here runs a network or billable command. No model provider keys and no modelProvider stanza appear: this artifact is the thin MCP-forwarding adapter (decision 13.0) hosted as a Node-based container (decision 13.12 option a), so R11 holds.

configure.$comment: Registers THIS containerized MCP server. Values mirror the `runtime` block above, which stays the single source of truth; the CLI `agentcore configure` step consumes these to scaffold the AgentCore Runtime registration.

pinnedVersions.$comment: Pin the CLI tool + container base image so `configure`/`launch` are reproducible (no ^/~). baseImage matches the FROM line in Dockerfile.

workflow.$comment: OPERATOR-run, `cloud-deploy`-gated (task 13.9); listed for documentation only — do NOT run as part of tests. `localTest` is the only network-free step and is wired to the agentcore tier tests.

env.$comment: Provided at deploy time (task 13.9); NOT baked into the image. No model provider keys (R11).

MCP_ENDPOINT.description: Cloudflare control-plane MCP Streamable HTTP endpoint. When unset, tools/call fails closed with HTTP 501.

AUTH_JWT_SECRET.source: AWS Secrets Manager (the same HS256 secret the Agent_Api signs with); server-side only, never in the image/build args/logs/responses (R15.7)
AUTH_JWT_SECRET.description: HS256 verification secret for the R15 Auth_Token inbound verifying layer (task 13.4).

inboundAuth.$comment: R15 Auth_Token reconciliation (task 13.4 / R15.1, R15.2, R15.3, R15.9 / Properties 1, 28, 29).

nativeJwtAuthorizer.reason: AgentCore's native customJWTAuthorizer requires an OIDC discoveryUrl + JWKS of asymmetric public keys (e.g. RS256). The R15 Auth_Token is a stateless SYMMETRIC HS256 JWT minted by the Agent_Api with a server-side shared secret (Decision 0.1) — there is no OIDC issuer, no .well-known discovery, and no JWKS for the native authorizer to fetch. It therefore cannot verify the HS256 token cleanly.

behavior.onMissingOrInvalidOrExpired: HTTP 401, no MCP forwarding, no Run_Manifest disclosure, non-disclosing error body (R15.1, R15.3 / Property 28)
behavior.onValid: establish Caller_Identity from verified claims before forwarding (R15.2 / Property 29)
behavior.authNeverSubstitutesApproval: true — every spend boundary stays gated at the Cloudflare Hitl_Gate_Service; this tier performs no paid action and no approval logic (R15.9 / Property 1)
